This Privacy Policy describes how and when Vweeter collects, uses, and shares your information when you use Vweeter, including our mobile applications and websites (the "Services"). Vweeter receives your information through our mobile applications, websites, email notifications, and other interactions with our Services. When using any of our Services you consent to the collection, transfer, manipulation, storage, disclosure and other uses of your information as described in this Privacy Policy. Irrespective of which country you reside in or supply information from, you authorize Vweeter to use your information in the United States and any other country where Vweeter operates.

You may remove address book data from Vweeter at any time through the settings in your Vweeter app. When you connect your Vweeter account to other services, you are able to post your Content to those services as well. You may disconnect your Vweeter account from your account on the other services at any time. Disconnecting your Vweeter account will immediately revoke our authorization to cross-post on your behalf or otherwise access that account and we will delete information obtained from that connection (other than information that was publicly posted on Vweeter, such as profile information), which may take some time.

Most Internet browsers automatically accept cookies. If you do not want cookies from Vweeter or other websites, you can instruct your browser, by changing its settings, to stop accepting cookies or to prompt you before accepting a cookie from the websites you visit. However, some Services may not function properly if you disable cookies.
